site_title: 주간 AI ｜ AI 활용 기계공학 논문 브리핑

ai.title: Overview of Risk Assessment and Management for Intelligent Systems under the AI Act and Beyond
ai.summary: The society and emerging risk-based regulatory frameworks for AI underscore the need for rigorous risk assessment to ensure safe and reliable AI systems. In response to this imperative, this paper presents an overview of AI risk assessment (identification and analysis) and management methodologies. It begins by reviewing the worldwide regulatory landscape that drives the need for systematic AI risk assessment. Then we characterize the spectrum of AI-related risks identified in the literature, from technical failures to ethical and social impacts. Subsequently, it reviews key risk assessment methodologies proposed for AI systems, focusing on general frameworks. The paper highlights best practices and illuminates methodological gaps, highlighting areas for further research on AI risk assessment.
ai.tldr: An overview of AI risk assessment (identification and analysis) and management methodologies and describes the spectrum of AI-related risks identified in the literature, from technical failures to ethical and social impacts.
ai.title_ko: AI 법규 및 그 이상을 고려한 지능형 시스템의 위험 평가 및 관리 개요
ai.summary_ko:
- AI 시스템의 안전과 신뢰성을 위해 엄격한 위험 평가의 필요성이 강조됩니다.
- 본 논문은 AI 위험 평가(식별 및 분석) 및 관리 방법론을 개괄적으로 다룹니다.
- 전 세계 AI 규제 환경을 검토하고 AI 관련 위험의 스펙트럼을 분석합니다.
- 기술적 오류부터 윤리적, 사회적 영향까지 다양한 AI 위험을 식별합니다.
- AI 위험 평가의 모범 사례와 방법론적 격차를 제시하며 추가 연구 분야를 조명합니다.

ai.title: Program-as-Weights: A Programming Paradigm for Fuzzy Functions
ai.summary: Many everyday programming tasks resist clean rule-based implementation, such as alerting on important log lines, repairing malformed JSON, or ranking search results by intent, and are increasingly outsourced to large language model APIs at the cost of locality, reproducibility, and price. We propose fuzzy-function programming: compiling such a function from a natural-language specification into a compact, locally-executable neural artifact. We instantiate this paradigm with Program-as-Weights (PAW), in which a 4B compiler trained on FuzzyBench, a 10M-example dataset we release, emits parameter-efficient adapters for a frozen, lightweight interpreter. A 0.6B Qwen3 interpreter executing PAW programs matches the performance of direct prompting of Qwen3-32B, while using roughly one fiftieth of the inference memory and running at 30 tokens/s on a MacBook M3. PAW reframes the foundation model from a per-input problem solver into a tool builder: invoked once per function definition, it produces a small reusable artifact whose subsequent calls per function application are cheap and offline.
ai.tldr: Program-as-Weights (PAW) reframes the foundation model from a per-input problem solver into a tool builder: invoked once per function definition, it produces a small reusable artifact whose subsequent calls per function application are cheap and offline.
ai.title_ko: Program-as-Weights: 퍼지 함수를 위한 프로그래밍 패러다임
ai.summary_ko:
- 일상적인 프로그래밍 작업 중 모호한 규칙 기반 구현은 LLM API에 의존하며 비용, 재현성 등의 문제가 있습니다.
- 이 논문은 자연어 사양으로부터 함수를 컴파일하여 작고 로컬에서 실행 가능한 신경망 아티팩트를 만드는 퍼지 함수 프로그래밍을 제안합니다.
- Program-as-Weights (PAW)는 4B 컴파일러가 FuzzyBench 데이터셋으로 학습되어 경량 인터프리터용 어댑터를 생성합니다.
- 0.6B Qwen3 인터프리터는 Qwen3-32B 직접 프롬프팅과 유사한 성능을 보이며, 메모리는 1/50, M3 MacBook에서 30 토큰/초로 실행됩니다.
- PAW는 파운데이션 모델을 문제 해결사에서 도구 빌더로 전환하여, 저렴하고 오프라인으로 재사용 가능한 아티팩트를 생성합니다.

mech.title: A Hybrid Physical Damage Neural Network for Wear Prediction of Self-Lubricating Bearings
mech.summary: Self-lubricating bearings are widely used in aerospace, marine, and other fields due to their excellent performance. Accurate wear prediction for self-lubricating bearings is crucial for ensuring the reliability and safety. However, achieving both physical interpretability and high accuracy in predictive models remains a challenge, as these bearings typically operate under varying load conditions and in high-noise environments. In this paper, a hybrid physical damage neural network is proposed for wear prediction. First, a “physics neuron operator” based on the Archard wear model is designed and embedded into the network to directly compute wear depth. Second, a cumulative damage law is introduced into this operator to quantify the degradation path of the bearing during operation. Finally, the evolution law of wear stages is encoded as a physical constraint in the loss function to compel the network's learning process to follow the true degradation mechanism. To validate the model, a dedicated test platform was built, and a full life-cycle degradation dataset for self-lubricating bearings was collected. Experimental results show that the proposed model significantly outperforms existing methods in prediction accuracy. Furthermore, this paper provides an in-depth analysis of the model's physical interpretability, revealing its internal working mechanism and significantly enhancing its credibility and generalization ability.
mech.title_ko: 자가 윤활 베어링 마모 예측을 위한 하이브리드 물리 손상 신경망
mech.summary_ko:
- 자가 윤활 베어링의 마모 예측은 신뢰성과 안전성 확보에 중요하나, 물리적 설명력과 정확도 동시 달성이 어렵습니다.
- 본 논문은 Archard 마모 모델 기반의 '물리 뉴런 연산자'를 신경망에 통합하여 마모 깊이를 직접 계산합니다.
- 이 연산자에 누적 손상 법칙을 도입하고, 마모 단계 진화 법칙을 손실 함수의 물리적 제약으로 활용합니다.
- 전용 테스트 플랫폼에서 수집한 전체 수명 주기 데이터를 통해 모델의 성능을 검증했습니다.
- 제안된 모델은 기존 방법보다 예측 정확도가 뛰어나며, 물리적 설명력과 일반화 능력을 향상시켰습니다.

mech.title: Compression of Polyconvex Envelopes of Isotropic Functions via Monotonic Input Convex Neural Networks
mech.summary: This work presents a novel neural-network compression approach for polyconvex envelopes of isotropic functions. The approach relies on a classical sufficient criterion for polyconvexity and is particularly suited for the representation of determinant-constrained energy densities arising in non-linear elasticity. Compared with existing compression methods based on the necessary and sufficient characterisation of polyconvex isotropic functions, the proposed framework reduces computational costs, due to the domain reduction through the restriction to the positive octant in the singed singular value space. The underlying neural-network architecture employs input-convex neural networks (ICNNs) with non-negative weight constraints to enforce the required convexity and monotonicity properties. The additional symmetry and inequality conditions characterising the polyconvex envelope are incorporated weakly through the loss function during training. Although the employed criterion is only sufficient and thus generally yields only a lower bound on the polyconvex envelope, numerical experiments based on the classical Saint Venant--Kirchhoff energy demonstrate that the proposed approach produces accurate approximations in practice while offering a computationally more efficient alternative to existing methods.
mech.title_ko: 등방성 함수의 다중 볼록 포락선 압축을 위한 단조 입력 볼록 신경망
mech.summary_ko:
- 이 연구는 등방성 함수의 다중 볼록 포락선(polyconvex envelopes)을 압축하는 새로운 신경망 접근법을 제시합니다.
- 제안된 방법은 다중 볼록성(polyconvexity)에 대한 고전적인 충분 조건을 활용하여 계산 비용을 줄입니다.
- 입력 볼록 신경망(ICNN)과 비음수 가중치 제약을 사용하여 볼록성 및 단조성을 구현합니다.
- 손실 함수를 통해 대칭 및 부등식 조건을 약하게 통합하여 학습을 진행합니다.
- 기존 방법보다 계산 효율적이며, Saint Venant-Kirchhoff 에너지에 대해 정확한 근사를 제공합니다.

papers.summary: The society and emerging risk-based regulatory frameworks for AI underscore the need for rigorous risk assessment to ensure safe and reliable AI systems. In response to this imperative, this paper presents an overview of AI risk assessment (identification and analysis) and management methodologies. The paper highlights best practices and illuminates methodological gaps, highlighting areas for further research on AI risk assessment.

papers.title: A Hybrid Physical Damage Neural Network for Wear Prediction of Self-Lubricating Bearings
papers.summary: Self-lubricating bearings are widely used in aerospace, marine, and other fields due to their excellent performance. papers.title: Compression of Polyconvex Envelopes of Isotropic Functions via Monotonic Input Convex Neural Networks
papers.summary: This work presents a novel neural-network compression approach for polyconvex envelopes of isotropic functions. The approach relies on a classical sufficient criterion for polyconvexity and is particularly suited for the representation of determinant-constrained energy densities arising in non-linear elasticity.
